calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 292619 Lai vs YANG.

These causes came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204. Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

Petitioners: Lun Wong and Yee Choy Lai appeared with Counsel Heidi Li and with Cantonese Interpreter Cynthia Li, Social Worker from Adult Protective Services.
Respondent: Shu Ou Yang (no appearance).

The Stiputlation That Commissioner May Act As Judge Pro Tempore was signed by Counsel and the Petitioners.
Interpreter was sworn in. Petitioners were sworn and examined.

The Court hears testimony, receives evidence and after hearing the evidence presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 50 yards away from the protected persons: Lun Wong and Yee Choy Lai, with the following exception as to the residence: as long as the parties continue to reside at the 580 Capp St., San Francisco, CA, there is a five yard stay away in effect. Other stay away order: Shu Ou Yang is prohibited from being on the 5th Floor while residing at 580 Capp St., San Francisco, CA or in the Activities Room concurrently.

Respondent shall not Physically Abuse, Financially Abuse, Intimidate, Molest, Attack, Assault (sexually or otherwise), Hit, Follow, Stalk, Threaten, Harass, Destroy Personal Property, Keep under Surveillance, or Block Movements, Contact (directly or indirectly), Telephone, Send Messages, Mail or e-Mail, Take Any Action, Directly or Through Others, to Obtain the Addresses or Locations of the Protected Persons.

The Defendant shall not own, possess, have, buy or try to buy, receive or try to receive, or in any other way get a gun or firearm.

Expires August 19, 2012.

Orders signed in open court, (one for each matter).
